articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5 in early April 2026, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a 1M-token context window. The model demonstrates significant improvements on graduate-level science benchmarks and complex multi-step planning tasks, narrowing the gap toward robust agentic workflows in enterprise settings.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Ultra 2 Achieves State-of-the-Art on Mathematical Olympiad Problems
articles.summary: DeepMind announced Gemini Ultra 2 can now solve over 90% of International Mathematical Olympiad problems end-to-end, a milestone that surpasses prior formal reasoning benchmarks by a wide margin. The system combines chain-of-thought search with a learned verifier model, suggesting a path toward reliable formal proof generation for research mathematics.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Begins Commercial Deployment of Figure 03 Humanoids in BMW Factories
articles.summary: Figure's third-generation humanoid robot has entered full commercial deployment at BMW's Spartanburg plant, handling parts kitting, inspection, and inter-station transport across three production lines. The robots use on-device vision-language models for task understanding and can be re-tasked via natural language instructions, reducing reprogramming time from days to minutes.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics Atlas Electric Demonstrates Autonomous Construction Site Work
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics showcased its electric Atlas performing multi-hour autonomous routines on an active construction site, including carrying heavy materials over uneven terrain and operating power tools. The demonstration highlights advances in whole-body manipulation and dynamic balance that bring general-purpose humanoid labor closer to economic viability in high-risk industries.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude's 'Extended Thinking' Mode for Multi-Hour Autonomous Research Tasks
articles.summary: Anthropic rolled out an extended autonomous research mode for Claude that can execute multi-hour investigative workflows, iteratively searching the web, reading papers, writing and running code, and synthesizing findings into structured reports. Early benchmarks show the system produces literature reviews on par with domain PhD students, raising both excitement and concerns about AI-driven research automation.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production on A14 2nm Process with GAA Transistors
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed volume production on its N2 process node using gate-all-around nanosheet transistors, delivering roughly 25% more performance at iso-power compared to N3E. Apple and NVIDIA are expected to be lead customers, with the node critical for next-generation AI accelerators that need higher transistor density to keep pace with model scaling demands.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Approval for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface Implant
articles.summary: The FDA granted Neuralink approval to begin broader clinical trials with its N2 implant, which doubles the electrode count to 2,048 channels and adds bidirectional stimulation capabilities. Early trial participants with spinal cord injuries have demonstrated cursor control speeds approaching able-bodied benchmarks, marking a significant step toward therapeutic brain-computer interfaces at scale.

articles.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Begins: First Compliance Audits Target Foundation Model Providers
articles.summary: The European Union's AI Office launched its first formal compliance audits under the AI Act, targeting major foundation model providers including OpenAI, Google, and Meta over transparency, safety testing, and copyright documentation requirements. The enforcement actions are being closely watched as a bellwether for global AI regulation and could establish precedents that shape how frontier models are evaluated and deployed worldwide.
